CI: patch homelab-k8s manifest with image SHA after docker build
description:
Update each docker workflow in hq-beadhub (discord-bridge, claude-agent, ai-dispatcher, beadhub) to add a patch-manifest job that runs after a successful build.

## What it should do
After the image is built and pushed, the job should:
1. Clone Woody88/homelab-k8s using GH_TOKEN (needs write access)
2. Replace the :latest image tag in the relevant manifest with :sha-${{ steps.meta.outputs.version }} (the sha- tag already produced by docker/metadata-action)
3. Commit and push to homelab-k8s main branch

## Manifest paths to patch
- discord-bridge: manifests/platform/beadhub/discord-bridge.yaml
- claude-agent: manifests/platform/beadhub/orchestrator.yaml (and worker job template in orchestrator-scripts.yaml if present)
- ai-dispatcher: manifests/platform/beadhub/ai-dispatcher.yaml
- beadhub: manifests/platform/beadhub/api.yaml

## Notes
- GH_TOKEN already has repo scope — verify it has write access to homelab-k8s, otherwise a separate HOMELAB_GH_TOKEN secret may be needed
- Use sed or yq to patch the image line — keep it simple
- git config user.email and user.name before committing (use github-actions[bot])
- Only patch if the build job succeeded (needs: build-and-push)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each workflow has a patch-manifest job
- [ ] After a merge to main, the relevant homelab-k8s manifest is updated with the new SHA tag
- [ ] Commit message includes the image name and SHA for traceability

title: ArgoCD: configure GitHub webhook for immediate sync on homelab-k8s push
description:
Configure ArgoCD to receive GitHub webhooks from homelab-k8s so it syncs immediately on push instead of waiting for the 3-minute polling interval.

## Background
- ArgoCD is exposed publicly via Cloudflare tunnel (all subpaths routed)
- The webhook endpoint is: https://<argocd-public-hostname>/api/webhook
- ArgoCD verifies webhooks using a shared secret stored in argocd-secret (key: webhook.github.secret)

## What needs to be done

### 1. Add webhook secret to argocd-secret (homelab-k8s repo)
Check how argocd-secret is managed in manifests/platform/argocd/ — likely via ExternalSecret.
Add the webhook.github.secret key to whatever secret source is used (Bitwarden/ExternalSecret or direct patch).

### 2. Register webhook on GitHub
In the homelab-k8s repo settings, add a webhook:
- Payload URL: https://<argocd-public-hostname>/api/webhook
- Content type: application/json
- Secret: same value as webhook.github.secret
- Events: Just the push event

### 3. Verify
After setup, push a commit to homelab-k8s and confirm ArgoCD syncs within seconds.

## Notes
- Find the ArgoCD public hostname from the Cloudflare tunnel dashboard config or existing ingress/route rules
- ArgoCD docs: https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/operator-manual/webhook/
- The argocd-secret may need a patch in kustomization — check existing patches in manifests/platform/argocd/patches/

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] webhook.github.secret is configured in argocd-secret
- [ ] GitHub webhook registered on homelab-k8s repo
- [ ] Push to homelab-k8s triggers ArgoCD sync within ~5 seconds
